description: You are given an array of integers and a target value. Find the two distinct elements in the array whose sum equals the target, and return their indices. You may assume each input has exactly one valid pair, and you cannot use the same element twice.
Given a signed 32-bit integer, reverse the order of its digits. If reversing causes the value to fall outside the signed 32-bit integer range, return 0 instead.

examples.explanation: Leading zero from reversal is dropped

description: Determine whether a given integer reads the same forwards and backwards (a palindrome), without converting it to a string.
Convert a Roman numeral string into its integer value. Roman numerals use symbols I, V, X, L, C, D, M, and follow subtractive notation in specific cases (e.g. IV = 4, IX = 9).
constraints: s contains only the characters I, V, X, L, C, D, M
Input is guaranteed to be a valid Roman numeral in range [1, 3999]

description: Given an array of strings, find the longest string that is a prefix of every string in the array. If no common prefix exists among all strings, return an empty string.
Given a string containing only the bracket characters (), {}, and [], determine whether every opening bracket has a matching closing bracket of the same type, in the correct order.
constraints: s consists only of the characters ()[]{}

description: You are given the heads of two sorted linked lists. Merge them into a single sorted linked list by splicing together the existing nodes, and return the head of the merged list.
constraints: Both lists are sorted in non-decreasing order
Combined node count is between 0 and 50

description: Given an array sorted in non-decreasing order, remove the duplicate values in place so each unique value appears only once, keeping the relative order. Return the count of unique values.
constraints: Array is sorted in non-decreasing order

description: Given an array and a value, remove all occurrences of that value in place, keeping the relative order of the remaining elements. Return the count of remaining elements.
Given two strings, a haystack and a needle, return the index of the first occurrence of the needle in the haystack. If the needle does not occur in the haystack, return -1.
